mechanism_narrative: Low and stable unemployment signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to robust corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods of UNRATE below 7% for more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% indicates monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:higher interest rates increase borrowing costs
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based weakening in consumer and investment demand, indicating a potential economic contraction that negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer and housing weakness signals recession
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show significant declines over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growing steadily indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns, especially during periods of moderate inflation.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth does not correlate positively with S&P 500 returns when CPIAUCSL annual growth is between 1.5% and 3.0% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 2.5% over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate revenues, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of periods following a significant decline in PAYEMS over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates stagflation, a challenging economic environment where corporate profits are squeezed and consumer demand weakens, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual % change > 4% AND UNRATE 3-month average change > 0.5pp
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 25% of periods exhibiting high inflation and rising unemployment over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and generally positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:strong retail sales boost corporate earnings
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat during periods of RSAFS 12-month growth > 3% for more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
